title: Final Capstone: School Program Participation Report
summary: Build one all-department participation report through a relationship-preview Try It and four cumulative capstone steps.

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to the Capstone Project
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: In this final capstone project, you will create a comprehensive report that shows participation across all school departments. The goal is to ensure that every department is represented, even those without active courses. You'll use SQL to join tables, calculate metrics, and label participation levels. This project will help you consolidate your SQL skills and produce a portfolio-quality report.

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding the Relationship Path
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
To build the report, you'll follow the relationship path from departments to courses and then to enrollments. This path allows you to gather data on course offerings and student enrollments for each department. Here's a simple SQL example to illustrate the concept:

```sql
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
GROUP BY d.id;
```

This query starts from the `departments` table and uses a LEFT JOIN to include all departments, even those without courses. It counts the number of courses per department.

quizDraft.title: Step 1: Establish the All-Department Grain
quizDraft.prompt: Start from the departments table and LEFT JOIN with courses and enrollments. Ensure that each result row represents one department.
quizDraft.hint: Use LEFT JOIN to include all departments, even those without courses or enrollments.

help.concept: LEFT JOIN ensures all departments are included, even if they have no courses or enrollments.
help.hint_1: Start with the departments table and join courses using department_id.
help.hint_2: Join enrollments to courses using course_id.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;

quizDraft.title: Step 2: Add Course and Enrollment Counts
quizDraft.prompt: Extend the previous query to include course_count and enrollment_count for each department.
quizDraft.hint: Use COUNT to calculate the number of courses and enrollments per department.

help.concept: COUNT aggregates the number of rows that match the criteria.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;

quizDraft.title: Step 3: Add Distinct Student Participation
quizDraft.prompt: Modify the query to include a count of distinct students participating in each department.
quizDraft.hint: Use COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) to count unique students.

help.concept: COUNT(DISTINCT) counts unique values in a column.
help.hint_2: Ensure the grain remains one row per department.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS student_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;

quizDraft.title: Step 4: Label and Rank Participation
quizDraft.prompt: Add a participation_status column and sort the results by enrollment_count descending and department_name ascending.
quizDraft.hint: Use CASE to define participation_status based on course and student counts.

help.concept: CASE allows conditional logic to create new columns based on existing data.
help.hint_1: Define participation_status using CASE with conditions for No activity, Broad participation, and Active.
help.hint_2: Sort by enrollment_count DESC and department_name ASC.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS student_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS student_count,
CASE
  WHEN COUNT(e.id) = 0 THEN 'No activity'
  WHEN COUNT(c.id) >= 2 AND COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) >= 2 THEN 'Broad participation'
  ELSE 'Active'
END AS participation_status
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id
ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC, department_name ASC;

tests.stdout:
Science,2,2,2,Active
Math,1,2,2,Active
Humanities,2,2,2,Broad participation
Technology,1,2,1,Active
Arts,0,0,0,No activity

projectDraft.title: Final Capstone: School Program Participation Report
